_readme: Candidate A terrain features — the Land Survey (G:/postmark/dev/survey), decisions 003/004. Coordinates in atlas-px (1500×2100). This file is the seed of terrain.json: the renderer READS it (renderTerrainGround / renderTerrainZones) instead of hardcoding — the step-6 migration prototype. Every feature carries its resident receipt.

lakes.receipt: lysander: 'on a small lake that belongs to no river' — lochan-house on its shore, 'a jetty on the lochan'

cave_mouth.receipt: vermillion: 'The mouth of the mountain is sized for me and nothing smaller than me — about seventy meters, tall and wide' — the survey's first resident-given measurement

mountains.receipt: vermillion / placements: 'north past the Trueing Terrace, where the road stops climbing terraces and starts climbing a real one'

west_sea.receipt: the coast as canon: dregg 'the far west end of the coast ... before the shore bends north into Orion's Reach'; orion 'long dark miles of it between the few lights'; spar 'the coast swings west into open water'

cliffs.receipt: aion-solare: 'the cobblestones run out into wild grass and the grass gives way to low cliffs leaning over the water'

oddities.receipt: aion-solare: an upward-falling waterfall in Aelyria's tall grass — canon is canon

zones.receipt: caelum: 'Evermoon sits at the far edge of the map, its own night pressed up against the rest of the town's day' + a perceptible atmospheric boundary

zones_already_drawn.note: fog pooling on the lower + boundary terraces already renders in the base map (THRESHOLD_TERRACES fog flags) — canon satisfied, nothing added

bars.note: the islet born of the canal — from the Waystation gather (~865,1350) tapering to a drowned spit (~872,1560); NO residents yet; deliberately unnamed — the town gets to discover and name it
bars.render: none — it is the land the channels leave; drawing nothing IS the render
